action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 5 tested the hybrid model locally without passing `env`, so the pathfinder was never triggered. Results were poor (avg_dist ~5m, safe_steps ~63-110). The first eval (iter 1) with a simpler GPS heuristic reached the human 56% of the time (nav_to_pos_success=0.56) but scored 0 seek success. Analysis: The hybrid model's pathfinder branch has a sign error (turn = +k*rel_yaw should be -k*rel_yaw because positive ang_vel = turn right/CW, and positive rel_yaw = target to the left). Additionally, using Euclidean distance for the near/far threshold fails when a wall separates robot and human; geodesic distance from the pathfinder is better. The local controller can also be made smoother and more responsive. Decision: Rewrite model.py to (1) fix pathfinder turn sign, (2) use geodesic_distance for the mode threshold, (3) use proportional forward control in local mode. Update test_hybrid.py to pass `env` so the pathfinder is actually exercised. Run a 2-episode local sanity test. Do NOT submit eval yet; inspect local results first.
